sections.heading: Complications
sections.content: Disease-related complications: Biliary obstruction, Gastric outlet/duodenal obstruction, Thromboembolic disease, Bleeding from primary tumor site, Pain, Depression and malnutrition. Treatment-related complications: Chemotherapy includes nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome, neuropathy; Immunotherapy immune-related adverse events; Targeted therapy includes fever, rash, dizziness, dysgeusia; Surgery includes pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage, infection; Radiation includes diarrhea, nausea, damage to duodenum, stomach, liver, kidneys, spinal cord.

labels: Bleeding from the primary tumor site

cards.name: Biliary obstruction
cards.detail: Endoscopic biliary metal stent (preferred), percutaneous biliary drainage, open biliary-enteric bypass [AMP-G]

cards.name: Gastric outlet/duodenal obstruction
cards.detail: For good PS: gastrojejunostomy ± G/J-tube or enteral stent; for poor PS: venting PEG tube or enteral stent [AMP-G]

cards.name: Thromboembolic disease
cards.detail: Low-molecular-weight heparin preferred over warfarin; consider direct oral anticoagulants for patients without luminal tumors [AMP-G]

cards.name: Bleeding from the primary tumor site
cards.detail: Therapeutic endoscopy, radiation therapy, angiography with embolization [AMP-G]

cards.name: Pain
cards.detail: Early referral to pain specialist; opioids with or without neurolysis; EUS-guided celiac plexus neurolysis; palliative RT; SBRT; high-intensity focused ultrasound; intrathecal drug delivery [AMP-G]

cards.name: Depression and malnutrition
cards.detail: Formal Palliative Medicine Service evaluation; nutritional evaluation with a registered dietitian; pancreatic enzyme replacement for exocrine pancreatic insufficiency [AMP-G]

cards.name: Nausea (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Vomiting (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Myelosuppression (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Mucositis (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Hand-foot syndrome (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Neuropathy (chemotherapy)
cards.detail: Toxicities vary by regimen; modified FOLFIRINOX is often modified by omitting bolus fluorouracil due to high toxicity [AMP-E footnote e]; Nausea, vomiting, myelosuppression, mucositis, hand-foot syndrome (capecitabine), neuropathy (oxaliplatin)

cards.name: Immune-related adverse events
cards.detail: Immune-related adverse events as per NCCN Guidelines for Management of Immune Checkpoint Inhibitor-Related Toxicities [AMP-E footnote j]

cards.name: Fever (targeted therapy)
cards.detail: Specific toxicities based on agent: e.g., dabrafenib/trametinib (fever, rash), entrectinib (dizziness, dysgeusia)

cards.name: Rash (targeted therapy)
cards.detail: Specific toxicities based on agent: e.g., dabrafenib/trametinib (fever, rash), entrectinib (dizziness, dysgeusia)

cards.name: Dizziness (targeted therapy)
cards.detail: Specific toxicities based on agent: e.g., dabrafenib/trametinib (fever, rash), entrectinib (dizziness, dysgeusia)

cards.name: Dysgeusia (targeted therapy)
cards.detail: Specific toxicities based on agent: e.g., dabrafenib/trametinib (fever, rash), entrectinib (dizziness, dysgeusia)

cards.name: Pancreatic fistula (post-surgery)
cards.detail: Pancreatoduodenectomy: morbidity 27-59%, mortality 2-10% [4,5,8,48,66,75-80]; Postoperative complications: pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage, infection

cards.name: Delayed gastric emptying (post-surgery)
cards.detail: Pancreatoduodenectomy: morbidity 27-59%, mortality 2-10% [4,5,8,48,66,75-80]; Postoperative complications: pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage, infection

cards.name: Hemorrhage (post-surgery)
cards.detail: Pancreatoduodenectomy: morbidity 27-59%, mortality 2-10% [4,5,8,48,66,75-80]; Postoperative complications: pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage, infection

cards.name: Infection (post-surgery)
cards.detail: Pancreatoduodenectomy: morbidity 27-59%, mortality 2-10% [4,5,8,48,66,75-80]; Postoperative complications: pancreatic fistula, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage, infection

cards.name: Diarrhea (radiation)
cards.detail: Acute gastrointestinal toxicity (diarrhea, nausea), potential damage to organs at risk (duodenum, stomach, liver, kidneys, spinal cord) [AMP-F]

cards.name: Nausea (radiation)
cards.detail: Acute gastrointestinal toxicity (diarrhea, nausea), potential damage to organs at risk (duodenum, stomach, liver, kidneys, spinal cord) [AMP-F]
